evidence.page_title: Kate Middleton and Meghan Markle reunite for the Festival of ...
evidence.title: The Cambridges and the Sussexes reunite for the Festival of Remembrance – best photos
evidence.description: The Duke and Duchess of Cambridge and the Duke and Duchess of Sussex joined the Queen and senior members of the royal family at the Festival of Remembrance at the Royal Albert Hall in London
evidence.text:
The Duke and Duchess of Cambridge and the Duke and Duchess of Sussex were reunited in public at the annual Royal British Legion Festival of Remembrance at the Royal Albert Hall in London on Saturday evening.
Prince William, Kate, Prince Harry and Meghan joined the Queen, the Prince of Wales and the Duchess of Cornwall at the event, which commemorates all those who have lost their lives in conflicts.
This year marks the 75th anniversary of a number of crucial battles of 1944 - Monte Cassino, Kohima and Imphal, and D-Day – with a particular emphasis on the role of the Commonwealth and Allied Forces.
Other senior members of the royal family who were in attendance were the Duke of York, Prince Edward and the Countess of Wessex, Princess Anne and Sir Timothy Laurence, the Duke and Duchess of Gloucester and the Duke of Kent.
READ: Why Sussexes and the Cambridges sit separately at the Festival of Remembrance
Click through our gallery to see all of the photos from the special night…
The Duke and Duchess of Sussex arrived, with Meghan wearing a chic black jacquard dress, believed to be a bespoke piece by Erdem, with her Aquazzura Deneuve bow pumps and new ruby earrings from Jessica McCormack.
MORE: Meghan Markle delights in black jacquard dress at the Festival of Remembrance
The Duke and Duchess of Cambridge were next to arrive, with Kate looking elegant in a dark blue dress with a fitted waistband and an embellished headband by high street favourite Zara. She also borrowed the Queen's pearl earrings.
MORE: Kate Middleton wears on-trend Zara headband for Festival of Remembrance
Prince Edward and the Countess of Wessex were among the first arrivals, with Sophie wearing a faux fur jacket and long black dress. Their daughter Lady Louise celebrated her 16th birthday on Friday.
The Queen's daughter, Princess Anne, arrived wearing a black outfit with a patterned scarf.
The Prince of Wales and Duchess of Cornwall make their entrance, with Camilla in a two-tone black dress. Camilla had to cancel her attendance at a gala dinner to mark the 200-year anniversary of Prince Albert's birth on Wednesday and had to miss visiting the Field of Remembrance at Westminster Abbey with Harry and Meghan on Thursday, due to a chest infection, Clarence House said.
The Queen was the last to arrive at the Festival, in line with the order of precedence. The Duke of Edinburgh was not in attendance, as he retired from public duties in 2017.
Harry and Meghan sat behind Prime Minister Boris Johnson and his girlfriend Carrie Symonds.
The royal family sat in the royal box, in the order of precedence, with the Queen, Charles, Camilla, William and Kate in the front row.
The Duke and Duchess of Cambridge clap during one of the performances. Kate wore a brooch with three poppies.
Harry and Meghan smile as they watch one of the performances.
Charles and Camilla clap during one of the performances.
The Queen wore her brooch with five poppies to watch the performances.
The royal family stand and applaud the performers and veterans at the Festival of Remembrance. The Queen will be joined by senior members of the royal family for the Remembrance Day service at the Cenotaph in Whitehall on Sunday.

evidence.page_title: Why Sussexes and the Cambridges sit separately at the Festival of ...
evidence.title: Why Sussexes and the Cambridges sit separately at the Festival of Remembrance
evidence.description: The Duke and Duchess of Cambridge get front row seats while the Duke and Duchess of Sussex can enjoy time out of the limelight
evidence.text:
It was a happy reunion on Saturday evening for the former Fab Four as the Duke and Duchess of Cambridge and the Duke and Duchess of Sussex stepped out together along with the rest of the royal family for the annual Festival of Remembrance at the Royal Albert Hall.
ALL THE PHOTOS: From the Festival of Remembrance
WATCH: The Sussexes and the Cambridges watch the performance
But while fans were delighted to see the two couples back together at the venue, once they stepped inside, Harry and Meghan took their seats on different sides of the royal box to William and Kate. The reason? The entire family was seated in order of precedence.
Harry and Meghan are seated further back in the royal box due to order of precedence
Her Majesty the Queen of course always takes centre position at events such as these, and since Prince William is second in line to the throne, he and Kate get the honour of siting to the monarch’s right hand side. Prince Charles - first in line - sits to his mother’s left, while Prince Harry sits in the row behind with his wife Meghan, close to Prime Minister Boris Johnston.
Harry is now sixth in line to the throne, behind William’s three children George, Charlotte and Louis. Prince Harry will have been delighted to have a date in his diary to catch up with his brother as he admitted recently that their paths were diverging slightly at the moment.
Duchess Meghan looked stunning in black jacquard dress
"We are certainly on different paths at the moment but I will always be there for him as I know he will always be there for me,” Harry told Tom Bradbury in his documentary Harry and Meghan: An African Journey. “We don't see each other as much as we used to because we are so busy but I love him dearly."
FASHION: Kate stuns in Zara headband at Festival of Remembrance
Tom also asked the Duke of Sussex whether news stories that the brothers had fallen out had any truth to them. The new dad didn't issue an outright denial, instead saying that "inevitably stuff happens" given the pressure of the roles they were born into. But he added that there was little truth to the speculation, saying: "The majority of the stuff is created out of nothing but as brothers, you know, you have good days, you have bad days.”
Kate looked stunning in blue with a Zara headband
William, Kate, Harry and Meghan all wore poppies at the annual Festival, which commemorates all those who have lost their lives in conflicts. This year marks the 75th anniversary of a number of crucial battles of 1944 - Monte Cassino, Kohima and Imphal, and D-Day – with a particular emphasis on the role of the Commonwealth and Allied Forces.
Other senior members of the royal family who were in attendance were the Duke of York, Prince Edward and the Countess of Wessex, Princess Anne and Sir Timothy Laurence, the Duke and Duchess of Gloucester and the Duke of Kent.

evidence.page_title: The Royal Family at the Festival of Remembrance 2019 Photos
evidence.title: The Royal Family Have Gathered to Pay Their Respects to Fallen Soldiers at the Festival of Remembrance
evidence.description: The Queen and her family honored those who died in battle at this somber event.
evidence.text:
All of the Photos of the Royal Family from the Festival of Remembrance
The Queen and her family honored those who died in battle at this somber event.
Every year on the second weekend in November, the royal family pay their respects to the soldiers who have died in battle during military conflicts in British history. As part of the homage, the Queen and members of her family gather for the Festival of Remembrance at the Royal Albert Hall which features musical performances to commemorate Britain's war heroes.
As well as Her Majesty, Prince Charles and Camilla, Duchess of Cornwall, Princess Anne, Prince William and Prince Harry and their wives, Kate Middleton and Meghan Markle were also in attendance to show their support. Here are all the best photos of the royal family on this solemn occasion.
Lauren Hubbard is a freelance writer and Town & Country contributor who covers beauty, shopping, entertainment, travel, home decor, wine, and cocktails.

evidence.page_title: Meghan et Harry ont-ils vraiment snobé William et Kate à la ... - Closer
evidence.title: Meghan et Harry ont-ils vraiment snobé William et Kate à la cérémonie du Souvenir ?
evidence.description: Ce samedi 9 novembre, Meghan Markle, Kate Middleton et leurs deux époux étaient présents à la cérémonie du souvenir qui s'est déroulée à la salle londonienne Royal Albert Hall. Mais les deux couples ne se sont pas mélangés.
evidence.text:
Ainsi, la reine Elizabeth était évidemment la mieux placée, et
occupait la place centrale. Elle était entourée de son fils
aîné Charles et de son épouse Camilla. Suivaient ainsi le prince
William et Kate Middleton, puisque l'aîné du prince héritier est
son successeur. Sont apparus ensuite les autres enfants de la
reine, parmi lesquels le prince Andrew, qui étaient accompagnés du
nouveau Premier ministre Boris Johnson, et de sa petite amie Carrie
Symonds. Meghan et Harry, eux, sont arrivés loin derrière,
en étant placés sur une rangée inférieure.

evidence.page_title: Meghan Markle Wore An Elegant Black Dress to the Festival of ...
evidence.title: Meghan Markle Wore An Elegant Black to the Festival of RemembranceMeghan
evidence.description: The Duchess of Sussex joined the royal family in honoring fallen soldiers today.
evidence.text:
Meghan Markle and her husband Prince Harry joined Queen Elizabeth, Prince Charles, and many other members of the royal family today for a somber event memorializing fallen soldiers. The Royal Festival of Remembrance, held annually at the Royal Albert Hall, is one of several remembrance events that the royal family will be involved in this weekend, including Remembrance Day on November 10.
This year's memorial celebration featured musical performances from artists including James Blunt, Leona Lewis, and Jeff Goldblum, alongside the Central Band of the Royal Air Force and the Band of HM Royal Marines, to mark the 75th anniversary of the battles of 1944, including D-Day, and the 100th anniversary of the founding of the intelligence and security organization, the Government Communications Headquarters.
To commemorate those lost, Meghan wore an understated yet elegant black fit and flare dress with a subtle brocade. Button details at the front and a scooped V-neck lapel added a vintage-esque touch which she matched with demure accessories; slingback heels, a black leather clutch, and gold hoop earrings.
Meghan accented the look with a red poppy pin, a century-old tradition in Britain and some of the Commonwealth countries, on her left shoulder. Though the flower, a symbol commemorating lost soldiers, is traditionally worn on the left for men, there's some debate about whether it should be worn on the right for women. However Meghan seems to have taken a cue from the Queen herself, as well as her sister-in-law Kate Middleton, and chosen to sport the poppy on the left.
In addition to tonight's event, Meghan and many of the other royals will be gathering at London's Cenotaph for the national memorial service tomorrow.
Lauren Hubbard is a freelance writer and Town & Country contributor who covers beauty, shopping, entertainment, travel, home decor, wine, and cocktails.
